category_title: Preschoolers with frequent inappropriate social behaviors (yelling, taking toys, crying)
minimal_context: Gregory is participating in free play time with his peers in the preschool classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for free play. Go pick a center and play with the other children.

STUDENT: (Walks over to a peer) I want those! (Takes the other child's toys)

TUTOR: Let's try to play together.

STUDENT: (Hides the toys behind his back) No!

TUTOR: Remember to use your words.

STUDENT: (Yells at the other child and starts crying) They are mine!

options.A: Allow Gregory to pick from a choice board of special activities (nature walk, helping dry and stack lunch trays, or doing Starfall on computer) for the last 10 minutes of school if the majority of his day was positive.
options.B: Remain available without forcing interaction during free play, first simply sitting near Gregory and then speaking quietly and gently touching him on the shoulder.
options.C: Act persistently without becoming angry or insulting him in public; ensure Gregory gets neither an irritated response nor an accusation, nor is called in for questioning, but instead remain very patient and restrained with him.
options.D: Maintain daily contact with Gregory throughout the day, ensuring consistent communication and presence during classroom routines and free play.
